rationale: The score is unchanged (6/10) and the substantive differences between the two evaluations are reworded restatements of the same profile (TypeScript/full-stack proficiency, seniority above the required level) plus minor reshuffling of which specific tech is flagged as 'not explicitly mentioned' (React/Nest.js vs. PostgreSQL) — ordinary run-to-run variation. The one genuinely new concern, 'high salary expectations,' is explicitly attributed to the candidate's senior experience rather than to the São Paulo location, and no concern, strength, or score shift keys off the country change. This is within normal noise, not a demographic penalty.
